title: Marsdenia forsteri
taxonomy: Marsdenia forsteri is classified as a species within the genus Marsdenia, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is native to Australia and is often found in eastern and southern regions. Marsdenia forsteri is a climber with glossy, dark green leaves and produces small, greenish-white flowers in clusters. The plant is commonly referred to as the 'native dogbane' due to its similarity in appearance to the dogbane plant. Marsdenia forsteri is a relatively rare species and is often found in specific habitats such as forests and woodlands.

intro: Marsdenia forsteri, also known as the Forster's milk vine or Forster's moonflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
